title: HTTP
description: HTTP protocol fundamentals: methods, semantics, headers, caching and protocol evolution.

questions.question: What is the difference between idempotent and safe HTTP methods? Give examples of methods that are safe, idempotent-but-not-safe, and neither.
Explain the main categories of HTTP status codes (1xx–5xx). When would you return 401 vs 403, and 400 vs 422?
How does HTTP caching work? Explain the roles of Cache-Control, ETag and Last-Modified, and describe what a conditional request (304 Not Modified) flow looks like.

HTTP is stateless — what does that mean in practice, and what mechanisms are used to maintain sessions on top of it? Compare cookie-based sessions with token-based approaches (e.g. JWT in an Authorization header).
Compare HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2 and HTTP/3. What problems does each newer version solve (e.g. head-of-line blocking, multiplexing), and how does the transport layer change in HTTP/3?
